intro: Agent Orchestration is a local-first control plane for AI agents: you configure agents as personas, wire them into workflows that hand work off to each other, and run those workflows from a web dashboard or straight from Slack — while a live monitor records every handoff with its cost and the guardrails that kept it safe. The deployed instance runs in demo mode, so agent message bodies are placeholder text; the orchestration around them — the graph, the handoffs, the per-step cost, the approval gates — is real.
outro: Compose agents, set the rails, and ship.

steps.intent: Opening card: explain what Agent Orchestration is and who it's for, over an animated graphic of agents handing work down a chain.

card.title: Agent Orchestration
card.subtitle: Configure AI agents. Wire them into workflows. Run them from the web — or from Slack.

steps.intent: Architecture card: one Node service serves the UI, runs the workflow engine and agent runtime, and persists everything to SQLite; Slack and the web UI are two front doors.

card.title: One service, two front doors
card.subtitle: Web UI and Slack both drive the same workflow engine

steps.intent: Establish the dashboard: a live websocket connection, the agent and workflow-template counts, and a running cost meter in the sidebar.
Show that a workflow is a directed graph of agents joined by conditional edges — here the Coder hands off to the Reviewer on an 'always' edge.
Open the agent roster to show the configured personas.
Each agent is a persona with a role, a model, the tools it may call, and the channels it listens on.
Open the Coder agent to reveal its full configuration in a drawer.
The guardrails section is the safety story: a spend cap in dollars, a tool-call ceiling, and explicitly blocked actions like rm -rf and force-push.
steps.highlight: Guardrails: cost · tool calls · blocked actions

steps.intent: Close the drawer, switch to the Research pipeline, give it a prompt, and run it — the handoff chain is recorded as messages.

pre.value: Compare Postgres and SQLite for our workload and recommend one.

steps.intent: The monitor records the run as a chain of messages — human to Researcher, Researcher to Synthesizer — each tagged with its direction and an estimated cost.
Run the Development pipeline instead — it pauses at Awaiting approval, because the Coder requires a human to sign off before it acts. In production that approval comes back from Slack.
The same workflows can be triggered from Slack: mention the bot, the request is signature-verified at the ingress endpoint, and the same cost guardrails apply.
Closing card over the agent-chain graphic settling.

card.title: Agent Orchestration
card.subtitle: Compose agents. Set the rails. Ship it.

prerequisites.seed: Deployed instance is seeded on first boot with 4 agents (Coder, Reviewer, Researcher, Synthesizer) and 2 workflow templates (Development pipeline, Research pipeline). No login. Runs in demo mode — agent outputs are placeholder echoes.
